description: Incompressible and compressible, laminar and turbulent flow of fluids. Classical and finite-difference analysis using differential and integral formulation of the continuity, momentum and energy equations. Application to ducts, plates, spheres, blades, pumps, turbines, lubrication, shockwaves, nozzles, diffusers and other mechanical engineering equipment.

optimized_prerequisites.prerequisites_text: M E 363, or graduate/professional standing, or member of Engineering Guest Students

prerequisites.prerequisites_text: M E 363, or graduate/professional standing, or member of Engineering Guest Students
